title: Lesson 7: Base Multiplication
subtopics: Times Tables up to 5x5 Mental Strategy
learningGoals: Multiply near base numbers with minimal writing.
Use pattern recognition for faster products.
Extend base multiplication to larger numbers.
coreIdeas: Teach one visual pattern at a time, then checkpoint immediately.
Use active recall: student states next step before final answer.
Keep board, voice, and feedback in one continuous loop.

workedExamples.question: Demo 1: Times Tables up to 5x5 Mental Strategy
workedExamples.method: I do -> We do -> You do
workedExamples.answer: Solved with board explanation, then student teach-back.

workedExamples.question: Demo 2: Table Patterns on the 9-Point Circle
workedExamples.answer: Solved mentally and verified with a quick check step.

starterPractice: Warm-up: one easy question on Times Tables up to 5x5 Mental Strategy.
Guided: one medium question on Larger Number Multiplication.
Independent: one challenge question on Method Summary.

teachingScript.subtopic: Times Tables up to 5x5 Mental Strategy
teachingScript.teacherLine: Scene 1: Times Tables up to 5x5 Mental Strategy. We will learn the pattern, test it, and apply it immediately.
teachingScript.boardAction: Board plan for Times Tables up to 5x5 Mental Strategy: concept snapshot -> worked move -> student turn.
teachingScript.checkpointPrompt: Exercise A: in one line, explain 'Times Tables up to 5x5 Mental Strategy' from Lesson 7.
teachingScript.microPractice: Micro-practice: solve one 20-second task on 'Times Tables up to 5x5 Mental Strategy', then explain your method in one sentence.

screenplay.subtopic: Times Tables up to 5x5 Mental Strategy
screenplay.teacherLine: Namaste {{studentName}}. Welcome to Lesson 7: Base Multiplication. We start with Times Tables up to 5x5 Mental Strategy and build momentum together.
screenplay.boardAction: Focus board: headline, one visual anchor, and today's key move for Times Tables up to 5x5 Mental Strategy.
screenplay.checkpointPrompt: Exercise A: in one line, explain 'Times Tables up to 5x5 Mental Strategy' from Lesson 7.
screenplay.fallbackHint: Micro-practice: solve one 20-second task on 'Times Tables up to 5x5 Mental Strategy', then explain your method in one sentence.

screenplay.subtopic: Times Tables up to 5x5 Mental Strategy
screenplay.teacherLine: Watch this carefully: I model Times Tables up to 5x5 Mental Strategy in two clean steps, then you repeat the logic.
screenplay.boardAction: Teacher demo on board for Times Tables up to 5x5 Mental Strategy, with one deliberate pause before final step.
screenplay.checkpointPrompt: Exercise A: in one line, explain 'Times Tables up to 5x5 Mental Strategy' from Lesson 7.
screenplay.fallbackHint: If confused, focus on the first transformation only.

screenplay.subtopic: Times Tables up to 5x5 Mental Strategy
screenplay.teacherLine: Your turn now: answer by voice or text. Say the next step, not only the final answer.
screenplay.boardAction: Pause tutoring speech, keep board clean, and wait for learner input.
screenplay.checkpointPrompt: Exercise A: in one line, explain 'Times Tables up to 5x5 Mental Strategy' from Lesson 7.
screenplay.expectedStudentResponse: Student gives next step with short reasoning.
screenplay.fallbackHint: Micro-practice: solve one 20-second task on 'Times Tables up to 5x5 Mental Strategy', then explain your method in one sentence.

screenplay.subtopic: Times Tables up to 5x5 Mental Strategy
screenplay.teacherLine: Challenge beat: teach this move back to me in one sentence and solve one fresh example.
screenplay.boardAction: Show one fresh challenge prompt while preserving prior board context.
screenplay.checkpointPrompt: Challenge: apply Times Tables up to 5x5 Mental Strategy on a new example and explain why your move works.
screenplay.expectedStudentResponse: Student explains method and completes challenge accurately.
screenplay.fallbackHint: Use the same first move as the demo, then finish calmly.

screenplay.subtopic: Times Tables up to 5x5 Mental Strategy
screenplay.teacherLine: Repair beat: let us slow down and rebuild just the first mistake point together.
screenplay.boardAction: Re-draw only the failing step with color emphasis and short verbal cue.
screenplay.checkpointPrompt: Repair: what is the first correct move for Times Tables up to 5x5 Mental Strategy?
screenplay.expectedStudentResponse: Student states the corrected first move.
screenplay.fallbackHint: Start from the friendly base or anchor point shown on the board.
